tool_args.label: BUSFORWARD 2017

payload.title: BUSFORWARD 2017
payload.publisher: New York City Department of Transportation
payload.description: 2017 Bus Forward Report from NYC DOT outlining plans for Select Bus Service expansion and local bus service improvements across New York City

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: NYC DOT

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: MTA
Citywide Transit Plan

payload.description: Citywide Transit Plan that will build on the 2009 BRT plan to expand the SBS network and identify complementary projects beyond BRT based on changes in transit use, land use, demographics and other factors

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.

tool_args.label: Select Bus Service Program

payload.description: Bus rapid transit program launched in 2007 to improve bus speeds, reliability and ridership

tool_args.label: Bus Forward Program

payload.description: Program to target bus corridor segments identified by looking at speed, reliability and long and slow trips, with planning and outreach over next year and implementation in 2018 and 2019

warnings: project.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: implemented_by.
project.partner_agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency is relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.raw_value_text: over 309,000 daily riders, 12% of city bus ridership

tool_args.label: Proposed SBS network total daily riders

payload.raw_value_text: over 800,000 bus riders would be on routes with fast and reliable service, over 32% of city bus ridership
payload.description: With addition of new proposed SBS routes, over 800,000 bus riders on routes with fast and reliable service

tool_args.label: $196M increased capital funding for SBS

tool_args.label: Cumulative bus lane miles 2016

payload.description: Cumulative bus lane miles in New York City in 2016

tool_args.label: 49.2 mile bus lane miles increase 2008-2012

payload.description: 49.2 mile increase in bus lane miles between 2008 and 2012

tool_args.label: 28 arduous neighborhood bus connections

payload.claim_text: There are 28 neighborhood connections where customers choose to take an arduous trip that takes longer than 30 minutes and is slower than 8mph, with over 900 people making the trip by bus on an average weekday
payload.description: MTA identified 28 neighborhood connections with arduous bus trips

tool_args.label: 2015 City Council legislation for SBS update

payload.description: City Council legislation in 2015, sponsored by Council Member Brad Lander, called for NYC DOT to develop an updated plan for implementing Select Bus Service lines in partnership with the MTA

tool_args.label: May 2017 City Council legislation for transit improvements

payload.description: In May 2017, the Council passed additional legislation, sponsored by Council Member Ydanis Rodriguez, calling for NYC DOT to include plans for transit improvements other than BRT and strategies for improving transit access

tool_args.label: SBS program launched a decade ago (circa 2007)

payload.description: The Select Bus Service program launched approximately a decade before 2017

tool_args.label: Local bus priority corridor implementation 2018-2019

payload.description: Expected implementation of first round of priority corridors in 2018 and 2019

tool_args.label: Bus priority toolkit tools

payload.description: Tools to improve bus service: off-board fare collection, all-door boarding, bus-only lanes, queue jumps, transit signal priority, wider stop spacing, bus route changes, improved curb regulations, concrete islands, curb extensions, real time bus arrival information, improved wayfinding, bus bulbs, enhanced station amenities, bus dispatching, updated relief locations

tool_args.label: Four key analyses for SBS and local bus improvements

payload.claim_text: NYC DOT and the MTA identified four key analyses that projects should help address: Access to rapid transit service, Long and slow bus trips, Slow bus speeds, Bus reliability

tool_args.label: 21 candidate SBS corridors identified

payload.claim_text: NYC DOT and the MTA have identified 21 candidate corridors across the city for the next phase of SBS

tool_args.label: Citywide Transit Plan based on 2015 legislation

payload.description: City Council legislation in 2015 called for NYC DOT to develop an updated plan for implementing SBS lines, leading to the Citywide Transit Plan

tool_args.label: Citywide Transit Plan based on May 2017 legislation

payload.description: May 2017 legislation called for NYC DOT to include plans for transit improvements other than BRT and strategies for improving transit access

tool_args.label: SBS program implemented by NYC DOT with MTA

payload.description: NYC DOT implements the Select Bus Service program in partnership with the MTA

tool_args.label: SBS program partnered with MTA

payload.description: NYC DOT implements SBS program in partnership with the MTA

tool_args.label: Bus Forward program implementation timeline

payload.description: Bus Forward program first round priority corridors expected implementation in 2018 and 2019

tool_args.label: NYC DOT and MTA partnership for bus service improvement

payload.description: The City and the MTA are committed to working together to improve bus service citywide

tool_args.label: Bus Forward addresses Broadway, Bronx corridor

payload.description: NYC DOT and the MTA will be addressing issues affecting bus speed on corridors such as Broadway from W 225th St to W 231rd St

tool_args.label: SBS program uses 21 candidate corridors

payload.description: NYC DOT and the MTA have identified 21 candidate corridors across the city for the next phase of SBS

tool_args.label: Citywide Transit Plan includes SBS program

payload.description: The Citywide Transit Plan will build on the 2009 BRT plan to expand the SBS network and identify complementary projects beyond BRT

tool_args.label: Bus Forward included in Citywide Transit Plan

payload.description: Bus improvements including the Bus Forward program will be included in the broader Citywide Transit Plan
